Header
The Problem

Why Builder Projects
Lose Money

Every builder faces the same problems. Most accept them as normal. They are not.
ROW 1: Budget overrun
01
Cost Leakage
Budget Overruns Found After Handover

Budgets are prepared once and never updated. By the time the overrun surfaces, the project is already done. There is nothing left to fix — only money already lost.

Tower A — Cost Tracker Excel · Apr
Civil Work ₹80L ₹1.04Cr
Finishing ₹42L ₹61L
Labour ₹36L ₹44L
Overrun ₹47L
ROW 2: Client calling (flipped)
📞
Anil Skyline — Client
12 missed calls today
Today
What is the slab status?Unanswered
When is handover? 9:42 AM
Send me progress photos 3 days late
Will update you soon
Operations
Client Calling for Updates Every Day

Property buyers and investors have zero visibility into what is happening on site. Every update requires a call, a photo search, and a manual summary — pulling the PM away from actual work.

02
ROW 3: Subcon overbilling
03
Cost Leakage
Subcontractor Billing Exceeds Actual Work

Subcontractors bill for more work than was executed. Without quantities linked to bills, overbilling passes undetected. Disputes happen after payment — when money is already spent.

Bill — Ramesh Civil No verification
Claimed: 4,200 sqft Unverified
Actual on site Unknown
Approved & paid ₹2.94L
Overbilled estimate 1,060 sqft
ROW 4: Labour unverified (flipped)
Site Register — Tower B, Day 18 Manual
Rajesh Kumar — Mason Off-site
Suresh Helper Not verified
Ahmed Painter Marked absent
Payroll auto-calculated Wrong data
Operations
Labour Attendance Unverified Across Towers

Supervisors mark registers manually. Workers who were never on site get counted. Payroll disputes follow every month — but by then the wages are already out the door.

04
ROW 5: No P&L — full width visual
05
Visibility
No Live P&L View Across Phases and Towers

Project cost is visible only after every bill is in — usually after handover. A ₹38L overrun that built up over six months shows up as one number at the end, with no point where action was possible.

Section header
Platform Features

Everything Builders Need. One Platform.

The four modules below cover where builder projects lose money — budgets, client visibility, subcontractor billing, and unverified labour costs.

ROW 1: Project Execution
⏲Project Execution

Deliver Projects On Time and Within Budget

Time and cost control are critical for builders and developers. SiteFlow’s project scheduling, Gantt charts, and cost-tracking tools enable you to set deadlines, monitor progress, and avoid budget overruns. Automate task allocation, track dependencies, and get alerts for potential delays — ensuring timely project delivery without sacrificing quality.

  • Instant Updates
  • Visual Documentation
  • Task and Progress Tracking

ROW 2: Client Transparency (flip)
📣Client Transparency

Enhance Client Communication and Transparency

Keep property buyers, investors, and stakeholders informed on every stage of the construction process without the need for constant follow-ups. With SiteFlow’s ERP software for builders and developers, real-time updates, progress photos, and milestone tracking are instantly visible to all authorized users.

  • Real-time updates shared automatically
  • Progress photos with geo-tag and timestamp
  • Milestone tracking visible to all stakeholders

👷Subcontractor Management

Subcontractor Work Tracking

Get quick updates of work done and work orders of subcontractors. A general contractor can never miss any update with the help of our ERP software. Every bill is matched to measured quantities before payment goes out — protecting your margins on every work order.

  • Assign everyday tasks
  • Manage Sub Contractors work order
  • Track Sub Contractors Work Progress

ROW 4: Labour and Material (flip)
🏛Resource Management

Labour and Material Tracking

Get to know what material is coming on site and what is going out. Along with it, get to know how many workers are working on site everyday. GPS attendance and face recognition eliminate ghost workers and payroll disputes across every tower.

  • Manage material warehouse
  • Track the in and out of raw material
  • Get labour attendance and generate salary slips

FAQ
FAQ
Questions Builders Ask Before Starting
Yes. SiteFlow is built for multi-tower and multi-phase projects. You can create separate project structures for each tower or phase, track budgets and progress independently, and view a consolidated P&L across all of them in one dashboard. Builders running G+12 and above with multiple blocks use SiteFlow to manage each tower separately while keeping a single view at the company level.
Site engineers enter measured quantities against each work order before a bill is approved. The system compares the subcontractor’s claimed quantity against the measured quantity and flags any discrepancy. Bills only move to payment after a multi-level approval. This process has helped builders catch overbilling worth lakhs before payment goes out.
Yes. You can share milestone progress, photos, and cost summaries with clients through a structured PDF report or a shared access view. Updates are generated automatically from site data — no manual compilation. This eliminates the daily calls and builds client trust without adding work to your project manager.
Yes. SiteFlow has direct integrations with both Tally and Zoho Books. Bills, expenses, and payments recorded in SiteFlow can sync automatically to your accounts software, eliminating double entry. The integration is available as an add-on and can be configured during onboarding.
Most builder companies go live within 3 days of signing up. The onboarding process covers account setup, adding projects and team members, and role-based training for your site engineers, project managers, and accounts team. A dedicated onboarding manager is assigned to guide your team through the process at no additional cost.
Yes. SiteFlow is mobile-first. Site engineers submit DPRs, record attendance, update task progress, and raise material requests directly from their phones. GPS attendance works offline and syncs when connectivity is restored. The app is available on Android and iOS and is designed for use in low-network conditions common on construction sites.
Yes. Every expense — material, labour, subcontractor, and equipment — flows into a live P&L the moment it is recorded. You can view profit and loss per tower, per phase, or across the entire project in one dashboard. There is no waiting for month-end or reconciliation — the numbers update in real time as your team works.
SiteFlow starts at Rs 12,000 per user per year on the Business plan, with a minimum of 3 users. The Business+ plan at Rs 15,000 per user per year includes advanced BOQ, procurement, warehouse, and payroll features. Enterprise pricing is available for larger companies with custom requirements. All plans include a dedicated onboarding manager and no credit card is required to start.
